reasons: Valir can push X.Borg away during Fire Missile pressure or before Last Insanity reaches priority targets.
X.Borg wants enemies to stay in his burn path, while Valir's spacing tools deny that.

proof.summary: X.Borg wants to walk forward and maintain burn pressure, while Valir can knock him back, slow him, and reset the spacing.
worksBestWhen: Valir saves knockback for X.Borg's forward commit.
Teammates avoid chasing through X.Borg's burn area.
failureCases: X.Borg flanks and forces Valir to react late.
Valir uses knockback before X.Borg commits.

reasons: Karrie can help burn through X.Borg's armor-based durability if she safely stacks attacks.
She is useful because X.Borg often has to stay exposed while applying pressure.

proof.summary: X.Borg depends on armor durability and sustained presence, while Karrie's repeated Lightwheel Mark triggers punish durable frontline targets.
worksBestWhen: Karrie keeps enough distance from Fire Missiles and Last Insanity.
The team protects Karrie during X.Borg's engage.
failureCases: Karrie cannot maintain attacks long enough to trigger Lightwheel Mark.

reasons: Lunox can pressure X.Borg's armor and punish him harder once he is armorless.
Her damage profile is useful into durable targets that cannot instantly reach her.

proof.summary: X.Borg wants to survive through Firaga Armor and re-equip later, while Lunox can threaten him with target-HP scaling damage and burst windows.
worksBestWhen: Lunox focuses X.Borg after armor is weakened or broken.
Lunox avoids standing in Fire Missile burn range.
failureCases: Lunox gets caught by Last Insanity.
X.Borg disengages armorless and safely re-equips armor.

reasons: Pharsa can pressure X.Borg from beyond his preferred burn range.
Long-range poke is useful for breaking armor before X.Borg can reach the backline.

proof.summary: X.Borg wants to walk forward with armor and burn enemies over time, while Pharsa can damage him from safer distance and force armor loss before the engage.
worksBestWhen: Pharsa has vision and frontline protection.
X.Borg is forced to approach through open space.
failureCases: X.Borg reaches Pharsa during her channel.
Pharsa uses ultimate without vision and gets flanked.

reasons: Franco can punish X.Borg after armor breaks or when X.Borg commits too far forward.
Suppression gives Franco's team a clean window to burst X.Borg before he resets.

proof.summary: X.Borg is most punishable when armorless or overcommitted, while Franco's suppression can hold him in place for team follow-up.
worksBestWhen: Franco targets X.Borg after Firaga Armor is broken.
Franco's team is ready to burst during suppression.
failureCases: Franco uses ultimate while X.Borg still has enough armor and backup.
X.Borg baits the suppression and re-engages after Franco's cooldown is spent.
